# Conclusions

## Outcome

Hopefully, you have determined what is

- the good,
- the bad, and
- the ugly

for *you*


## Core Rust References

- [Rustup](https://rustup.rs/)
- [The Rust Programming Language](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/)
- [Rust By Example](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rust-by-example/)
- [The Cargo Book](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/)
- [The Rust Standard Library](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/)


## Crates Used In This Course

- [docs.rs](https://docs.rs/)
- [crates.io](https://crates.io/)
- [`clap`](https://docs.rs/clap/latest/clap/)
- [`serde`](https://docs.rs/serde/latest/serde/)
- [`csv`](https://docs.rs/csv/latest/csv/)
- [`toml`](https://docs.rs/toml/latest/toml/)
- [`rand`](https://docs.rs/rand/latest/rand/)
- [`rand_distr`](https://docs.rs/rand_distr/latest/rand_distr/)
- [`num-complex`](https://docs.rs/num-complex/latest/num_complex/)
- [`rayon`](https://docs.rs/rayon/latest/rayon/)


## Scientific Computing Crates

- [`ndarray`](https://docs.rs/ndarray/latest/ndarray/)
- [`nalgebra`](https://docs.rs/nalgebra/latest/nalgebra/)
- [`faer`](https://docs.rs/faer/latest/faer/)
- [`mdarray`](https://docs.rs/mdarray/latest/mdarray/)


## Further Rust References

- [The Rust Reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/)
- [The Rust API Guidelines](https://rust-lang.github.io/api-guidelines/)
